name: WI-779-E: Converge legacy-identity-table READERS (account/consent/billing non-v2) to v2-only — unblocks WI-1139
description: Scope: identity-cutover-779-strip proposal (_wip/identity-cutover/2026-07-01-identity-cutover-779-strip-proposal.md) §5 steps 1-2, operator-ratified R3 plan (ic-orch-352). Un-parked: the imagined operator-gated terminal cutover this WI was waiting on does not exist (§4/§6) — there is only a mechanical cleanup. Unblocks WI-1128 (FK repoint) and, transitively, WI-1139 (schema-def deletion) and WI-779's closure.
acceptanceCriteria:
Scope: proposal §5 steps 1-2 only.

Step 1 — collapse the metering.ts identityV2 ternary to v2-only. apps/api/src/services/billing/metering.ts: decrementQuota (~:259), safeRefundQuota, getEffectiveAccessForSubscription — remove the legacy branch AND the identityV2 param. This is the single load-bearing behavioural change in the whole 779-strip lane (§7.1): verify the v2 branch is a complete replacement (quota decrement, refund, effective-access) and that collapsing the param does not silently change any caller's behaviour.

Step 2 — remove legacy readers + relocate shared symbols. Compiler-driven, NOT blind file deletion — a legacy module can be DB-reader-dead yet still export error classes / route-facing types / parse helpers that v2 code or tests import (e.g. apps/api/src/routes/consent.ts:20,39 imports legacy consent error classes while calling the v2 consent service).
  2a. Delete/rewrite the code paths that read/write legacy tables (profiles/accounts/subscriptions/family_links/consent_states). Targets: packages/database/src/account-repository.ts; legacy services/billing/* superseded by services/billing-v2/*; legacy stripe-webhook-handler.ts / revenuecat.ts superseded by their -v2 twins.
  2b. Before deleting each legacy impl file, move any still-used shared errors/types/parse-helpers it exports into neutral (non-legacy) modules.
  Guard every file deletion: rg -n "<symbol|basename>" apps packages tests → pnpm typecheck → targeted tests.

Acceptance (done at outcome level, per §6 correction — not blind deletion):
- No production code reads or writes a legacy table (profiles/accounts/subscriptions/family_links/consent_states) via Drizzle.
- Every remaining import from a legacy module is either removed or relocated to a neutral module — no orphaned imports, pnpm typecheck clean.
- A fresh independent sweep confirms no reader was missed: real Drizzle-table references, not word-grep (the \b(profiles|...) pattern false-matches English prose) — explicitly covering inngest jobs and any raw-SQL FROM subscriptions / FROM profiles sites (§7.3).

Out of scope (other WIs in the lane): FK repoint + migrations + seed conversion (WI-1128); schema-def deletion (WI-1139); dev reseed + final verify (WI-779 parent).

§0 framing applies: zero production users, non-prod data disposable — no mode-pin/drain/soak/runbook ceremony; reversible via git.
